LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN REED'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE DECEMBER 17, 2018 ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT TRANSPORTATION BROKERAGE SPECIALISTS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS. A R G U E D. COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE DECEMBER 17, 2018 ORDER IS VACATED AND A NEW ORDER IS ENTERED DENYING TBS'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. FURTHER, THE COURT STAYS THE CASE FOR THE PURPOSE OF DISCOVERY THAT IS BROADER THAN WOULD BE AVAILABLE IN ARBITRATION. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE:HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: W. Order signed.

Chad Saunders, Esq., Hunter Pyle Law, Telephone 510-444-4400 & Alex Tovarian, Esq., Law Offices of Alex Tovarian, Telephone 415-984-9990, appearing for Plaintiff;
Trevor R. Witt, Esq., Roxborough Pomerance, et al, Telephone 818-992-9999, appearing for Defendant Transportation Brokerage Specialist,

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as modified:

Plaintiff John Reed's motion for reconsideration of the December 17, 2018 order granting defendant Transportation Brokerage Specialists, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted. The December 17, 2018 order is vacated and a new order is entered denying TBS's motion to compel arbitration. Zakaryan v. The Men's Wearhouse, Inc. (2019) 33 Cal. App. 5th 659 constitutes "new or different law" within the meaning of CCP 1008(a). Even if it did not, Mr. Reed's motion is treated as an invitation to the court to reconsider the December 17, 2018 order and the court exercises its discretion per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 to do so. When TBS' motion to compel arbitration was heard and decided on December 17, 2018, the only on-point authority binding on this court on the issue of whether Mr. Reed's PAGA claim is arbitrable was Esparza v. KS Industries, L.P. (2017) 13 Cal. App. 5th 1228 which required this court to grant TBS' motion. Per CRC 8.1115(e)(1), because a petition for review had been granted and was pending, Lawson v. ZB, N.A. (2018) 18 Cal. App. 5th 705 had "no binding or precedential effect." With the issuance of Zakaryan, there are now two on-point authorities with full precedential effect, but neither is binding on this court because Esparza and Zakaryan reach conflicting results based on conflicting reasoning and are not reconcilable. This court "must make a choice between the conflicting decisions." (Auto Equity Sales, Inc. v. Superior Court (1962) 57 Cal. 2d 450, 456). Because Zakaryan is better-reasoned and comports better with Iskanian v. CLS Transportation Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal. 4th 348 than Esparza, this court chooses to follow Zakaryan rather than Esparza. Zakaryan requires that TBS' motion to compel arbitration be denied. Further, The Court stays the case for the purpose of discovery that is broader than would be available in arbitration .

Order is signed.

Judge:Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/HEK)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Set For Case Management Conference On Nov-13-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Trevor R. Witt, Esq.
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chad Saunders, Esq., Hunter Pyle Law, Telephone 510-444-4400, appearing for Plaintiff;
Trevor R. Witt, Esq., Roxborough Pomerance Nye & Adreani, Telephone 818-992-9999, appearing for Defendant Transportation Brokerage Specialists;

Ruling - ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Transportation Brokerage Specialists, Inc's motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending the completion of the arbitration of plaintiff John Reed's individual, victim-specific claims. Mr. Reed has not shown that Transportation Brokerage waived its right to seek arbitration of Mr. Reed's victim-specific claims. Transportation Brokerage's delay in seeking to compel arbitration was not unreasonable in light of the circumstances of this case nor did Transportation Brokerage engage in litigation conduct inconsistent with its right to seek enforcement of the parties 'arbitration agreement. (St. Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195 ("a party who resists arbitration on the ground of waiver bears a heavy burden, and any doubts regarding a waiver allegation should be resolved in favor of arbitration.")). Transportation Brokerage consistently informed Mr. Reed of its intent to seek arbitration of Mr. Reed's victim-specific claims and the pendency of efforts to mediate the dispute is a reasonable explanation for the delay. Mr. Reed has not shown that Transportation Brokerage took advantage of judicial discovery procedures not available in arbitration because Transportation Brokerage did not propound formal discovery, advised Mr. Reed that before any formal discovery starts the parties need to resolve the issue of arbitration of victim-specific claims, and answered a set of discovery propounded by Mr. Reed with objections for the purpose of preserving them. Transportation Brokerage's only pleading was an answer to the complaint in which it stated as an affirmative defense the arbitration agreement, and it did not file any other pleadings or motions and did not propound any discovery unlike defendants in the cases relied on by Mr. Reed. Per Esparza v. KS Industries, L.P. (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1228, which is currently binding on-point authority, Mr. Reed's claims for recovery of unpaid wages seek individual victim-specific relief that is covered by the enforceable arbitration language in the parties' agreement. In the interest of judicial economy and to avoid possibly inconsistent results, Mr. Reed's representative claims for PAGA civil penalties are stayed pending the resolution of the arbitration proceedings. Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
